title: Releases | Beehive.govt.nz
content:
## Maritime NZ board appointees confirmed
Associate Transport Minister James Meager has confirmed two new appointments to the board of Maritime New Zealand, including a new Chair and Deputy Chair.

## SH3 Mangapepeke culvert replacements now complete
Two new culverts have now been completed on State Highway 3 (SH3) north of New Plymouth, as part of the Government’s priority bridge replacement programme.

## Delivering a fairer electricity market for Kiwi consumers
New Zealanders will benefit from new rules announced by the Electricity Authority to create a level playing field in the electricity market.

## Loans to help businesses transition away from gas
The Government is introducing a $1.2 billion Gas Transition Loan Guarantee Scheme to help businesses reduce reliance on gas, protect jobs, and free up limited gas supplies, alongside support for energy efficiency and improved market transparency.

## Anti-stalking laws take effect at midnight
Stalkers will face the full force of the law when the clock strikes midnight tonight, Justice Minister Paul Goldsmith says.

## English language requirement extended to AEWV skill level 3 roles
Minimum English language requirements under the Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV) are being extended to skill level 3 roles, Immigration Minister Erica Stanford says.

## AIP Visa Growth Category expands to philanthropy
Investor migrants using the Active Investor Plus Growth category will soon be able to include a philanthropic component as part of their overall investment in New Zealand.

## Government boosts funding to tackle wilding pines
The Government is investing in rural productivity by supercharging efforts to tackle the spread of wilding pines, Biosecurity Minister Andrew Hoggard says.

## Budget 2026 shores up maritime security
The Government is investing in drone systems, critical ship maintenance and work to replace our aging naval fleet as it bolsters New Zealand’s maritime defensive and offensive capability.

## Funding for new tools to beef up border security
Budget 2026 is making a significant investment in Customs’ equipment and people to better protect New Zealand’s borders and to stop illegal drugs and organised crime.
